children.heading: Post-consumer materials management infrastructure grant program
children.label: a Post-consumer materials management infrastructure grant program
A
children.text: to support improvements to local post-consumer materials management, including municipal recycling programs; and

children.label: B
children.text: to assist local waste management authorities in making improvements to local waste management systems.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 2 Applications
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under paragraph (1), the applicant State shall submit to the EPA Administrator an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the EPA Administrator may require.

children.label: i
children.text: the project or projects to be carried out using grant funds; and

children.label: ii
children.text: how the project or projects would result in the generation of less plastic waste;

children.label: B
children.text: a description of how the funds will support disadvantaged communities; and

children.label: C
children.text: an explanation of any limitations, such as flow control measures, that restrict access to reusable or recyclable materials.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the activities carried out under this subsection;

children.label: B
children.text: estimates as to how much plastic waste was prevented from entering the oceans and other waterways as a result of activities funded pursuant to this subsection; and

children.label: C
children.text: a recommendation on the utility of evolving the grant program into a new waste management State revolving fund.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The EPA Administrator may provide competitive grants to units of local government, Indian Tribes, and public water systems (as defined in ) to support improvements in reducing and removing plastic waste and post-consumer materials, including microplastics and microfibers, from drinking water or sources of drinking water, including planning, design, construction, technical assistance, and planning support for operational adjustments.section 300f of title 42

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 2 Applications
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under paragraph (1), an applicant shall submit to the EPA Administrator an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the EPA Administrator may require.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The EPA Administrator may provide grants to municipalities (as defined in section 502 of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act ()) or Indian Tribes that own and operate treatment works (as such term is defined in section 212 of such Act ()) for the construction of improvements to reduce and remove plastic waste and post-consumer materials, including microplastics and microfibers, from wastewater.33 U.S.C. 136233 U.S.C. 1292

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 2 Applications
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under paragraph (1), an applicant shall submit to the EPA Administrator an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the EPA Administrator may require.

children.label: A
children.text: to support projects to reduce the quantity of solid waste in bodies of water by reducing the quantity of waste at the source, including through anti-litter initiatives;

children.label: B
children.text: to enforce local post-consumer materials management ordinances;

children.label: C
children.text: to implement State or local policies relating to solid waste;

children.label: D
children.text: to capture post-consumer materials at stormwater inlets, at stormwater outfalls, or in bodies of water;

children.label: E
children.text: to provide education and outreach about post-consumer materials movement and reduction; and

children.label: F
children.text: to monitor or model flows of post-consumer materials, including monitoring or modeling a reduction in trash as a result of the implementation of best management practices for the reduction of plastic waste and other post-consumer materials in sources of drinking water.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 2 Applications
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under paragraph (1), an applicant shall submit to the EPA Administrator an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the EPA Administrator may require.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The EPA Administrator shall ensure that all laborers and mechanics employed on projects funded directly, or assisted in whole or in part, by a grant established by this section shall be paid wages at rates not less than those prevailing on projects of a character similar in the locality as determined by the Secretary of Labor in accordance with subchapter IV of chapter 31 of part A of subtitle II of title 40.

children.heading: Authority
children.label: 2 Authority
children.text: With respect to the labor standards specified in paragraph (1), the Secretary of Labor shall have the authority and functions set forth in Reorganization Plan Numbered 14 of 1950 (; 5 U.S.C. App.) and .64 Stat. 1267section 3145 of title 40

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: 3 Requirements
children.text: The requirements of section 608 of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act () shall apply to the construction of a project carried out, in whole or in part, with assistance made available under this section in the same manner as the requirements of such section apply with respect to funds made available pursuant to title VI of such Act [ et seq.].33 U.S.C. 138833 U.S.C. 1381

children.heading: Limitation on use of funds
children.label: f Limitation on use of funds
children.text: A grant under this section may not be used (directly or indirectly) as a source of payment (in whole or in part) of, or security for, an obligation the interest on which is excluded from gross income under .section 103 of title 26
for the program described subsection (a), $55,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025; and1So in original. Probably should be followed by “in”.1
for each of the programs described subsections (b), (c), and (d), $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025.1
